headline: Freeport and Newmont Get a Boost. Why Mining Stocks are Rising.
summary: The stock prices of large mining companies rallied early Wednesday, after The Wall Street Journal reported on a U.S.-drafted peace plan as the conflict with Iran continues.  Shares of  Freeport-McMoRan Royal Gold  and  Newmont  all rose by 2.1%, 1.4% and 3.2%, respectively, early in the session.  Continuous gold futures  increased by 3.5% to $4,557.80 an ounce, while  silver futures  rose by 3.9%.

headline: Freeport Declares Quarterly Cash Dividends on Common Stock
summary: PHOENIX, March 25, 2026--Freeport (NYSE: FCX) announced today that its Board of Directors declared cash dividends of $0.15 per share on FCX’s common stock payable on May 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 15, 2026. The declaration includes a base dividend of $0.075 per share and variable dividend of $0.075 per share in accordance with FCX's performance-based payout framework. The payment of dividends is at the discretion of the Board, which will consider FCX's financial results, cash

headline: Trump's 15-Point Iran Plan Could Trigger A Snapback Rally In These 10 War-Battered Stocks
summary: The U.S. reportedly sent Iran a 15-point peace plan covering missiles, nuclear programs, and Hormuz maritime routes. Polymarket prices a 48% chance of a ceasefire by April 30. These 10 Russell 1000 stocks — down 17% to 33% since the war began — are first in line for the rebound.
